title: How Processed Food Hacks Your Brain
subtitle: 加工食品如何操纵你的大脑

paragraphs.en: If you're someone who has turned to snacking on junk food more in the pandemic, you're not alone. Investigative reporter Michael Moss says processed food is engineered to hook you, not unlike alcohol, cigarettes, or other harmful substances. His 2013 book, Salt Sugar Fat, explored food companies' aggressive marketing of those products and their impact on our health. In his new book, Hooked, Moss updates the food giants' efforts to keep us eating what they serve.
paragraphs.zh: 如果你是在疫情期间更多转向垃圾食品的人，你并不孤单。调查记者迈克尔·莫斯指出，加工食品被精心设计来让你上瘾，与酒精、香烟或其他有害物质无异。他2013年的著作《盐糖脂》探讨了食品公司对这些产品的激进营销及其对健康的影响。在新书《上瘾》中，莫斯更新了食品巨头为让我们持续消费其产品所做的努力。

paragraphs.en: Processed food is inexpensive, legal, and everywhere. Companies' advertising cues us to remember those products, and we want them constantly. So the food environment is one of those key things that makes food even more problematic for so many people. Memory, nostalgia in particular, plays a big role in the foods we crave. Soda companies discovered that if they put a soda in a child's hands at a ball game with their parents, that soda will forever be associated with that joyous moment.
paragraphs.zh: 加工食品便宜、合法且无处不在。公司的广告提示我们记住那些产品，让我们不断渴望它们。因此食品环境是使食品对许多人更具成瘾性的关键因素之一。记忆，尤其是怀旧，在我们渴望的食物中扮演重要角色。汽水公司发现，如果他们在孩子与父母观看球赛时将汽水递到孩子手中，那瓶汽水将永远与那个欢乐时刻绑定。

paragraphs.en: Later in life, when that child wants to experience a joyous moment, they're going to think of soda. Many people seek comfort in the snacks they remember from childhood. Moss examines the way companies capitalize on our memories, cravings and brain chemistry to keep us snacking. One of the reasons I came to think that some of these food products are even more powerful than drugs can be is memory. What we eat is all about memory, and we begin forming memories for food at a really early age.
paragraphs.zh: 日后当那个孩子想体验欢乐时刻时，就会想到汽水。许多人在童年记忆中的零食里寻求慰藉。莫斯剖析了公司如何利用我们的记忆、渴望和大脑化学来让我们不停吃零食。让我觉得某些食品甚至比毒品更具威力的原因之一就是记忆。我们吃什么全关乎记忆，而我们很早就开始形成对食物的记忆。

paragraphs.en: And we keep those memories for a lifetime. Knowing this, the food industry spends lots of time trying to shape the memories we have for their products. One of the features of addiction that scientists discovered in the 1990s was that the faster a substance hits the brain, the more apt we are to act impulsively. There's nothing faster than food in its ability to hit the brain. For Moss, this puts the notion of 'fast food' in an entirely new light, as nearly 90 percent of food products in grocery stores are processed foods.
paragraphs.zh: 而这些记忆会伴随我们一生。深知这一点，食品行业花费大量时间试图塑造我们对其产品的记忆。20世纪90年代科学家发现成瘾的一个特征是，物质抵达大脑的速度越快，我们就越容易冲动行事。没有什么比食物更快地冲击大脑了。对莫斯来说，这让“快餐”的概念焕然一新，因为杂货店里近90%的食品都是加工食品。

paragraphs.en: Everything in the industry is about speed, from manufacturing to packaging. Overall, Moss outlines the industry's dependence on making their products inexpensive, super delicious, and incredibly convenient for consumers. Now that more people care about what they eat and want to eat healthier, these companies find it difficult to meet that new demand because of their own addiction to making these convenience foods.
paragraphs.zh: 从制造到包装，该行业的一切都与速度有关。总体而言，莫斯概述了该行业对使产品廉价、超级美味且对消费者极其方便的依赖。如今越来越多的人关心自己吃什么并希望吃得更健康，这些公司却发现很难满足这种新需求，因为他们自己对制造这些方便食品已经上瘾。
